beginner.question: What is the 'hero section' in a Netflix-like streaming UI?
beginner.answer: The hero section is a large featured banner at the top of the page showing a currently promoted movie/show. It has a background image/video, title, description, and 'Play' / 'More Info' buttons. Typically 80-100vh tall.

beginner.question: What is a 'row carousel' in a Netflix clone?
beginner.answer: A row carousel is a horizontally scrollable row of content thumbnails (movies/TV shows), organized by category (Trending, Continue Watching, New Releases). Each row has a title and scrollable content strip with forward/backward arrows.

beginner.question: How do you create a responsive thumbnail card for a streaming service?
beginner.answer: Use a consistent aspect ratio (typically 2:3 for posters, 16:9 for wide thumbnails). Use `aspect-ratio: 2/3; object-fit: cover;`. Cards scale with the row. Desktop: 200-250px wide. Mobile: 120-150px wide.

beginner.question: What is the 'hover effect' on streaming thumbnails?
beginner.answer: On hover, thumbnails scale up (typically 1.3-1.5x), add shadow, show additional info (title, rating, year, buttons), and sometimes play a preview video. Use `transform: scale()` with `transition`, and `z-index` to overlay adjacent items.

beginner.question: What is the 'overlay' effect on the hero section?
beginner.answer: The hero overlay is a gradient (usually bottom-to-top or both sides) that darkens the background image so text over it remains readable. Example: `background: linear-gradient(to top, rgba(0,0,0,0.9) 0%, transparent 100%)`.

beginner.question: How do you build a responsive navigation for a streaming service?
beginner.answer: A top navbar with logo (left), category links (center), and user menu (right). On mobile, category links collapse into a hamburger menu. The navbar becomes transparent on top of the hero and solid on scroll. Use `position: fixed`.

beginner.question: What is the 'category row' structure in a Netflix clone?
beginner.answer: Each category row contains a label (e.g., 'Trending Now') and a horizontally scrollable container of thumbnails. The row uses `overflow-x: auto` with `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` for smooth scrolling.

beginner.question: How do you style a 'Play' button for a streaming hero?
beginner.answer: A prominent button with large padding, white background, black text (or inverse), and an SVG play icon. On mobile: slightly smaller but still touch-friendly. Use `display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;`.

beginner.question: What colors are typically used in a Netflix-like UI?
beginner.answer: Dark backgrounds (#141414, #181818, #222), white text (#fff, #e5e5e5), red accent (#e50914 for branding/hover), and gray for secondary text (#808080). The dark theme reduces eye strain for video browsing.

beginner.question: What is the purpose of the 'More Info' button on the hero?
beginner.answer: The 'More Info' (or details) button opens a modal/drawer with additional details about the featured content: synopsis, cast, genres, ratings, and related recommendations. It's semi-transparent to differentiate from the primary Play button.

intermediate.question: How do you implement a smooth horizontal scroll for Netflix-style rows?
intermediate.answer: Use `overflow-x: auto; scroll-behavior: smooth; scroll-snap-type: x mandatory;` with `scroll-snap-align: start` on thumbnails. Hide scrollbar with `::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }`. Implement left/right arrow buttons for desktop.

intermediate.question: How do you create the thumbnail hover scale effect without affecting layout?
intermediate.answer: Use `transform: scale(1.3); transform-origin: center; transition: transform 0.3s ease;` with `z-index: 1` on hover (`z-index: 10` on hover). The parent container needs adequate padding/margin to accommodate the scaled card.

intermediate.question: How do you implement responsive thumbnail row sizing?
intermediate.answer: Use CSS custom properties for thumbnail width: `--card-width: clamp(120px, 15vw, 250px);`. Set `flex: 0 0 var(--card-width)` on each card. Number of visible cards varies by viewport: 6 on desktop, 3 on tablet, 2 on mobile.

intermediate.question: How do you create the Netflix 'top 10' badge on thumbnails?
intermediate.answer: Use `position: absolute` on the thumbnail container with a numbered badge (1-10). Style as a large bold number with a subtle background or outline. Use `z-index` to layer above the image. Adjust size for mobile thumbnails.

intermediate.question: How do you implement a 'billboard' hero with video background?
intermediate.answer: Use `<video>` with `autoplay muted loop playsinline`. Overlay with gradient. Show title and buttons. On mobile, use a static image instead of video (reduce data usage). Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.

intermediate.question: How do you create the Netflix 'scroll-down' gradient effect on the navbar?
intermediate.answer: Initially, navbar has `background: transparent`. On scroll past hero, add a class `.scrolled` with `background: #141414`. Use `transition: background 0.3s ease`. In CSS, `.navbar { transition: background 0.3s; }`.

intermediate.question: How do you implement the 'My List' toggle button?
intermediate.answer: A button with a checkmark (+) icon. Toggle between 'Add to My List' and 'Remove from My List' states. Use `aria-label` for accessibility. Style with semi-transparent background and hover fill. Store state in JavaScript.

intermediate.question: How do you design a responsive 'content details' modal?
intermediate.answer: Desktop: centered modal (max-width: 900px) with hero image, synopsis, cast, related titles. Mobile: full-screen modal with vertical scrolling. Close button top-right. Use `overflow-y: auto`. Trap focus for accessibility.

intermediate.question: How do you handle responsive font sizes for the hero title?
intermediate.answer: Use `clamp()`: `font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 6vw, 4rem)`. The title should be large and bold. On mobile, the title shrinks but remains prominent. Adjust line-height for multi-line titles.

intermediate.question: How do you create animated skeleton loaders for streaming thumbnails?
intermediate.answer: Use `aspect-ratio` containers with a shimmer gradient: `background: linear-gradient(90deg, #222 25%, #333 50%, #222 75%); background-size: 200%; animation: shimmer 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;`. Row layout mirrors real content.

advanced.question: How do you implement Netflix-style 'click to expand' row (the row that expands on click)?
advanced.answer: When a user clicks a thumbnail, the row's height expands to show title, description, cast, and action buttons below the thumbnail. Use `max-height` transition or `grid-template-rows` transition for smooth expansion. Collapse other open rows.

advanced.question: How do you create a 'preview autoplay' video on thumbnail hover?
advanced.answer: Use a `<video>` element hidden behind the thumbnail poster. On hover, play the video (muted, low volume). On mouse leave, pause and reset to poster. Use `IntersectionObserver` to only load videos for visible rows.

advanced.question: How do you implement keyboard navigation for Netflix-style rows?
advanced.answer: Use arrow keys to navigate between rows (up/down) and within rows (left/right). Maintain a focus state on the current thumbnail. Use `tabindex="0"` on each card and programmatic focus management. Trap focus within rows.

advanced.question: How do you create a responsive 'genre carousel' with multi-row layout?
advanced.answer: Desktop: 2-3 rows of thumbnails visible at once in a vertically scrollable category section. Mobile: single row, scroll vertically between genres. Use CSS Grid with `grid-auto-flow: column` and `grid-template-rows: repeat(3, 1fr)`.

advanced.question: How do you implement responsive 'continue watching' progress bars on thumbnails?
advanced.answer: An absolute-positioned bar at the bottom of the thumbnail showing watch progress. Use `width: var(--progress)%` with `height: 3px; background: red;`. The `<figure>` or card wrapper has `position: relative;`.

advanced.question: How do you create a 'user profile switcher' UI?
advanced.answer: Desktop: dropdown with profile avatars and names. Mobile: full-screen profile grid. Each profile is a clickable card with avatar image. 'Add Profile' option at the end. Use CSS Grid for responsive layout. Active profile has a check indicator.

advanced.question: How do you implement 'maturity rating' badges on thumbnails?
advanced.answer: Absolute-positioned badges on thumbnail corners: TV-MA, PG-13, R, etc. Use `position: absolute; top: 8px; left: 8px;` with `background: rgba(0,0,0,0.8); color: white; padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 2px; font-size: 0.75rem;`.

advanced.question: How do you implement responsive 'audio/subtitle' selector UI?
advanced.answer: A modal or dropdown listing available audio languages and subtitle options. Desktop: dropdown from a button. Mobile: bottom sheet. Use `<ul>` with radio-style selection. Show checkmark next to current selection.

advanced.question: How do you create a Netflix-style 'title screen' with multiple background images?
advanced.answer: Use CSS `background-image` with multiple backgrounds: a primary hero image and a gradient overlay. `background: linear-gradient(to right, rgba(0,0,0,0.8) 0%, transparent 50%), url('hero.jpg') center/cover no-repeat;`.

advanced.question: How do you implement 'Lazy Loading' for thumbnail images below the fold?
advanced.answer: Use `loading="lazy"` on all thumbnail images not in the initial viewport. Use IntersectionObserver to load images when they enter the viewport. Show placeholder/shimmer until loaded. Use `will-change: transform` for smooth reveal.
